reviewScope: Re-entry review. Only change: GAP-001 fix added info callout (lines 37-41) documenting clone-specific permission requirements. All pre-existing content unchanged.

invariantResults.evidence: No Unicode en dashes (U+2013) or em dashes (U+2014) found anywhere in the file. No double-hyphen dashes used in the new callout content. File is clean.
New callout line 39 uses 'you need' phrasing: 'To clone a page, you need *Read*...' - matches the existing callout pattern on line 19. No instances of 'must' directed at people anywhere in the file.
New callout content is not indented (correct for top-level Liquid block content). All existing indented lines (e.g., list sub-items on line 29) use 4-space indentation consistently.

New callout uses active voice throughout: 'you need *Read*... on the source page, and *Read* and *Create* page permissions on the parent page where you want to place the clone.' Subject (you) performs the action.
No em dashes found in the file.
No UI element names appear in the new callout. Permission names (*Read*, *Create*) correctly use italics per INV-style-075.
Permission values *Read* and *Create* correctly italicized on line 39, matching the pattern on line 19.
New callout is a single concise sentence covering both source and target permission requirements.
No 'please' found in the file.
New callout uses identical terminology and structure as the existing callout: 'To [verb] a page, you need [permissions] on the [target].' Consistent use of 'page permissions' terminology throughout.
No pronoun 'it' in the new callout content.
No formal forms requiring contraction in the new callout content.
New callout leads with the condition: 'To clone a page, you need...' - condition-first pattern.
Info callout correctly used for additional context about permission requirements. Severity appropriate - not a warning or tip.
Single-sentence callout - title exception applies. Matches existing callout on lines 17-21 which also omits a title.
No callout type name ('Info') written inside the callout box.
Info callout used correctly for supplementary context about permissions.
No new headings added. Existing headings ('Create a new page', 'Clone a page') use sentence case correctly.
No 'have to' or 'has to' found in the file.
New callout is self-contained - no pronouns that escape the callout scope.
File starts with YAML front matter between --- delimiters.
All required front matter fields present: title, identifier (LwKQC), order (100), redirect_from (x/LwKQC), persona (business), license (1).

New callout uses correctly matched {% info %} / {% endinfo %} tags (lines 37, 41). Blank line after opening tag (line 38) and before closing tag (line 40). Identical structure to existing callout on lines 17-21.
Pre-existing images on lines 26 and 50 use border=true. No new images added.
Front matter includes related_pages: ['JwKQC', 'MAKQC'].
In-page link on line 13 correctly uses inpage_link. Cross-page links correctly use page_link.
New callout's page_link on line 39 uses correct format with identifier, anchor, and linkText attributes. Matches the existing cross-reference pattern on line 19.
Identifier 'permissions_pagelevel_xp' reused from existing callout on line 19 - not fabricated. Same anchor and linkText used.
Link text 'page permissions' is descriptive and matches the existing pattern. All link texts in the file are descriptive.
The new callout adds a centralized permission reference for the clone operation, cross-referencing the permissions page. This addresses the scattered-information concern.
Content documents current product state. No migration or version-specific language.
No 'Next steps' section added.
Page has title ('Create pages'), introduction (line 13), and body (two how-to sections with procedures).
Introduction on line 13 provides context about pages and links to content overview.
Permission callouts mention requirements upfront without blocking the happy path.

additionalFindings.evidence: Two h2 headings: 'Create a new page' (line 15) and 'Clone a page' (line 35). No skipped levels.

New callout (lines 37-41) structurally identical to existing callout (lines 17-21): same tag format, same blank-line pattern, same sentence structure ('To [verb] a page, you need [permissions] on the [location]'). Permission values consistently italicized.

All Liquid tags well-formed: info/endinfo pairs matched, page_link tags have required identifier and valid attributes, image and icon tags syntactically correct, inpage_link correctly used for in-page reference.
No trailing whitespace on lines 37-41 (new content).
ANTI-002 (dash-type confusions): No dashes of any kind in new content. No other anti-patterns triggered.

STYLE-004 (page_link identifier verification): New callout reuses 'permissions_pagelevel_xp' identifier from the existing callout on line 19 - same identifier, same anchor, same linkText. No fabrication risk.
